title: Stronger Engagement for Indian Health Needs Act of 2025
short_title: Stronger Engagement for Indian Health Needs Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill changes the job title of the person who leads the Indian Health Service. Instead of being called the Director, they will now be called the Assistant Secretary for Indian Health. This person will still do the same job of overseeing health care for Native Americans, but they will have a higher rank in the government. The new title means they will report directly to the Secretary of Health and Human Services and get paid the same as other Assistant Secretaries.
subjects: Department of Health and Human Services
Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Stronger Engagement for Indian Health Needs Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Assistant Secretary for Indian Health
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Indian Health Care Improvement Act. Right now, the person who runs the Indian Health Service is called a Director. This law changes that job title to Assistant Secretary for Indian Health. The Assistant Secretary will report directly to the Secretary of Health and Human Services, just like the Director did before. The person who is currently serving as Director when this law passes will automatically become the Assistant Secretary. The Assistant Secretary can hire a Deputy Assistant Secretary and other workers including lawyers to help run the Indian Health Service. Any time federal laws or government documents mention the Director of the Indian Health Service, those references will now mean the Assistant Secretary instead. This law also changes the pay level for this position. The job will be paid at the same level as other Assistant Secretaries in the Department of Health and Human Services.
